title: Initial Balance Extension Vs True Breakout
focus_questions: Did the IB extension become accepted outside trade, or was it only a poke beyond the range?
Was the session definition clear before promoting the extension into a breakout?

summary.thesis: An IB extension is only the first observation; a true breakout requires outside acceptance, hold quality, and a session-aware opening context.
summary.operator_translation: Do not trade the IB headline. Trade the accepted outside branch or the failed extension return.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when opening-auction structure and initial-balance statistics are being used to justify a breakout or reversal narrative.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: U.S. regular session opening auction with a defined initial balance. Required evidence: clear IB definition, extension beyond IB, and proof of either outside acceptance or return inside. Do not trade: from the first IB extension alone or by mixing overnight and RTH logic carelessly. Real trigger: accepted outside hold after IB extension or confirmed false-break return back inside IB. Failure point: the market proves the opposite branch on the first meaningful retest.

when_relevant: The U.S. session has a defined initial balance.
Price trades outside that balance and the operator is tempted to call breakout or failure.
Opening inventory, gap, or prior value context still matters to the branch.
required_evidence: the initial-balance definition being used is explicit
outside trade beyond IB can be separated from accepted hold quality
re-entry into IB or continuation outside IB can be observed after the extension
confirmation_signals: price extends outside IB and does more than print a one-touch poke
the first retest outside IB holds or the market clearly accepts a new auction there
follow-through supports the outside branch instead of instantly returning inside
invalidation_signals: the extension quickly returns inside IB and cannot hold outside
the session definition is ambiguous and the IB label is being misused
outside acceptance is being assumed before it is visible
no_trade_conditions: Do not trade IB statistics alone without current opening context.
Do not call the first extension a breakout before outside acceptance is visible.
Do not mix overnight range logic and RTH initial-balance logic without defining which session is active.

entry_archetypes.name: held outside IB retest
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the IB extension retest holds and outside trade is accepted.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the market only tagged outside and never built there.

entry_archetypes.name: failed IB extension return
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the IB extension fails and price is accepted back inside the opening range.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the return inside is only brief and outside hold is still unresolved.

management_notes: IB extension is an observation; breakout is a later judgment.
If the extension fails and price re-enters IB, downgrade the breakout narrative quickly.
If outside trade holds, shift the script from opening reaction to session expansion.
review_questions: Did the operator trade the IB headline or the outside acceptance after it?
Was the session definition clear before using the IB read?
After extension, did price build outside or fall back into the old opening structure?
